# scripts/run_all_top1pct_audits.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-12T10:00:40Z

## Purpose
Consolidated top-1% audit runner · runs all iter audits in one process.

Replaces 20 separate `audit_top1pct_iterN.py` invocations with one
in-process run · ~5× faster · single summary report.

Usage:
  python3 scripts/run_all_top1pct_audits.py                # run all
  python3 scripts/run_all_top1pct_audits.py --only 30,33   # only specified iters
  python3 scripts/run_all_top1pct_audits.py --since 25     # iters ≥ 25
  python3 scripts/run_all_top1pct_audits.py --json         # JSON output for CI

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import argparse
- from `_` import importlib
- from `_` import io
- from `_` import json
- from `_` import logging
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import sys
- from `_` import time
- from `contextlib` import redirect_stdout

## Inputs
- CLI args: --only, --since, --json, --verbose

## Process
### Functions
- `discover_iters()`: Find all audit_top1pct_iterN.py · return [(n, modname)].
- `run_one(modname)`: Run one iter audit · capture stdout · return summary dict.
- `main()`: (no docstring)

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Parse the "Summary: X/N pass · Y fail" line.
- FIX: match "Summary:" prefix only · per-step lines also contain
- "pass · http=200" detail suffixes that previously false-positive matched.
- "10/10 pass · 0 fail"
- Pretty summary
